title: Decisiv, PACCAR, Isuzu, Hino and KPIT Launch the SRM Alliance
author: Not Specified
description: Decisiv announced the formation of the SRM Alliance, an industry-wide initiative bringing together OEMs, dealers, and technology partners including PACCAR, Isuzu Commercial Truck of America, Hino Trucks, and KPIT to develop next-generation service management capabilities. The alliance aims to automate service processes, enhance workflows, and improve integration across dealership systems to increase efficiency and reduce fleet downtime.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Founding member of the SRM Alliance, demonstrating commitment to collaborative innovation in service management and positioning to benefit from improved service efficiency and customer retention.

title: The Market Is Flat for the Year but These Industrial Stocks Are Soaring
author: Matthew Benjamin
description: Industrial stocks in construction and farm equipment are significantly outperforming the broader market in 2026. Caterpillar, Deere, and Paccar have all posted double-digit gains, driven by strong earnings, urban expansion, infrastructure development, and demand from AI data center buildouts. The heavy construction equipment market is expected to grow 6.2% annually through 2034, while agriculture equipment is projected to grow 8.6% annually through 2035.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock up 28% in 2026 with stellar Q4 earnings (18% revenue growth, EPS of $5.16 vs. expected $4.71). Gas turbine division surging 23% due to AI data center demand. Company expects to double power generation equipment sales by 2030.

Stock up 25% in 2026. Agriculture equipment market moving out of down cycle with projected 8.6% annual growth through 2035. Company positioned to benefit from infrastructure expansion and economic growth.

Stock up 16% in 2026. Q4 results beat analyst expectations with adjusted EPS of $1.06 on revenue of $6.8 billion. Benefits from strong construction and transportation demand driven by infrastructure and AI buildout.

title: Why Paccar Stock Dropped Today
author: Rich Smith
description: Paccar stock fell 1.9% despite beating Q4 earnings expectations, as the semi-truck manufacturer reported significant year-over-year declines. Full-year 2025 sales dropped 16% to $28.4 billion and earnings collapsed 43% to $4.51 per share. With no guidance for improvement and analysts forecasting only 5% long-term earnings growth, investors remain skeptical despite the company's strong free cash flow position.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite beating earnings expectations by a penny, Paccar reported significant deterioration in fundamentals with Q4 sales down 14% YoY and full-year earnings down 43% YoY. The company provided no guidance suggesting improvement, and analysts forecast only 5% long-term earnings growth. The stock is trading at 26x trailing earnings while shrinking, making it unattractive despite adequate free cash flow generation.

title: Vocational Truck Market Worth USD 122.89 Bn by 2034 | Towards Automotive
author: Towards Automotive
description: The global vocational truck market is projected to grow from $62.04 billion in 2025 to $122.89 billion by 2034, with a CAGR of 7.89%. Dump trucks dominate the market, while concrete mixer trucks show the fastest growth. North America leads the market, with Asia-Pacific expected to experience the fastest expansion.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Known for high-quality products, advanced technologies, and commitment to innovation
Expanding production facilities in the US, indicating strategic market growth plans

title: Box Truck Market to Worth USD 24.31 Bn by 2034 | Towards Automotive
author: Towards Automotive
description: The global box truck market is expected to expand from USD 12.91 billion in 2025 to USD 24.31 billion by 2034, driven by e-commerce growth, sustainability initiatives, and technological advancements in logistics vehicles.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Specializes in lightweight, maneuverable urban delivery trucks with a focus on utility and affordability
Manufactures durable, high-performance box trucks with advanced technology features for long-haul deliveries
